message:
feat(fastapi-core): BackgroundTasks improvements and timing tests

- Add execute_with_context() for cancellation-aware task execution
- Add execute_with_panic_isolation() for isolated task execution
- Add format_panic_message() helper for panic payload extraction
- Add 5 timing tests verifying post-response execution behavior
- Tests cover: single task, queue order, task spawning, request
  isolation, and non-blocking concurrent request handling

Closes: fastapi_rust-5bf, bd-1ktc

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1hu2","title":"Phase 4: HTTP & Router Integration","description":"## Feature: HTTP & Router Integration

### Parent Epic
Rich Output Integration: Premium Console UI with Agent Compatibility (bd-nww8)

### Overview
This phase integrates rich output capabilities directly into the HTTP parsing and routing layers of fastapi_rust, enabling beautiful debug output for request/response inspection, routing decisions, and validation errors.

### Background & Reasoning
The HTTP and Router crates are the core of request handling. Rich output integration here provides:
- **Parser diagnostics**: Visual HTTP request/response inspection for debugging
- **Routing debug**: See exactly how routes are matched
- **Validation errors**: Beautiful validation failure messages
- **Performance profiling**: Visual timing breakdowns

Unlike application-level output, these components need careful integration to avoid performance impact on the hot path.

### Key Principles for HTTP Layer Integration

1. **Zero Cost When Disabled**: Rich output must have zero runtime cost when not actively debugging
2. **Feature-Gated**: HTTP debug output behind feature flag
3. **No Allocations on Hot Path**: Debug formatters only allocate when actually rendering
4. **Deferred Rendering**: Collect data, render later (not during request processing)

### Components in This Phase

#### 1. HTTP Request Inspector
Visual display of parsed HTTP requests showing:
- Method, path, query string
- Headers in a table
- Body preview (truncated)
- Timing information

#### 2. HTTP Response Inspector
Visual display of HTTP responses showing:
- Status code with color
- Headers
- Body preview
- Size information

#### 3. Routing Debug Output
Shows the routing decision process:
- Which routes were considered
- Why matches failed/succeeded
- Parameter extraction results
- Middleware applied

#### 4. Validation Error Display
Beautiful validation error messages:
- Field path highlighting
- Expected vs received values
- Nested error support
- Multiple errors grouped

### Technical Architecture

#### Feature Gating
```toml
# Cargo.toml
[features]
default = []
debug-output = ["fastapi-output"]
```

```rust
#[cfg(feature = "debug-output")]
pub fn debug_request(req: &Request) {
    RequestInspector::new(req).display();
}

#[cfg(not(feature = "debug-output"))]
pub fn debug_request(_req: &Request) {
    // No-op when feature disabled
}
```

#### Deferred Rendering Pattern
```rust
// Collect data during request processing
struct RequestDebugInfo {
    method: Method,
    path: String,
    headers: Vec<(String, String)>,
    body_preview: Option<String>,
    parse_duration: Duration,
}

// Render only when needed
impl RequestDebugInfo {
    pub fn display(&self) {
        let output = RichOutput::global();
        if !output.is_debug_enabled() {
            return;
        }
        // ... render
    }
}
```

### Integration Points

| Component | Integration Point | Output Type |
|-----------|------------------|-------------|
| fastapi-http | Parser completion | Request inspector |
| fastapi-http | Response building | Response inspector |
| fastapi-router | Route matching | Routing debug |
| fastapi-core | Extractor validation | Validation errors |

### Success Criteria
- [ ] HTTP debug output works without affecting production performance
- [ ] Feature flag properly gates all debug functionality
- [ ] Request/response inspection is visually clear
- [ ] Routing debug helps understand matching logic
- [ ] Validation errors are developer-friendly
- [ ] All output respects agent mode

### Dependencies
- Phase 3 complete (bd-1lkf) - builds on advanced components
- RichOutput facade (bd-36uw)
- FastApiTheme (bd-14v4)
- Access to fastapi-http and fastapi-router internals

### Tasks
1. Implement HTTP request inspector
2. Implement HTTP response inspector  
3. Implement routing debug output
4. Implement validation error display","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:14:51.046983969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:35:19.736470706Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:35:19.736398652Z","close_reason":"Implemented HTTP request/response inspectors, routing debug output, and enhanced validation error display in fastapi-output","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hu2","depends_on_id":"bd-1lkf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1lkf","title":"Phase 3: Advanced Output Components","description":"## Feature: Advanced Output Components

### Parent Epic
Rich Output Integration: Premium Console UI with Agent Compatibility (bd-nww8)

### Overview
This phase builds on the core components to add sophisticated visualizations for complex framework internals: middleware stacks, dependency trees, test results, and graceful shutdown progress. These components showcase rich_rust's advanced renderables (Tree, Progress, nested Panels) while maintaining strict agent compatibility.

### Background & Reasoning
Advanced developers need visibility into framework internals during development and debugging:
- **Middleware Stack**: Understanding execution order is critical for debugging auth/logging issues
- **Dependency Tree**: Visualizing DI resolution helps identify circular deps and optimization opportunities  
- **Test Results**: Beautiful test output increases developer happiness and productivity
- **Shutdown Progress**: Graceful shutdown feedback prevents confusion about whether the app is hung

### Components in This Phase

#### 1. Middleware Stack Visualization
Display the middleware execution order as a visual stack/tree showing:
- Middleware name and type
- Execution order (outside-in for requests, inside-out for responses)
- Configuration details
- Whether middleware short-circuits

#### 2. Dependency Tree Display
Visualize the dependency injection graph showing:
- Root dependencies
- Nested/transitive dependencies
- Circular dependency detection with clear error display
- Resolution order

#### 3. Test Results Formatter
Premium test output showing:
- Test name with pass/fail status (checkmarks/X marks)
- Timing information
- Grouped by module/file
- Summary statistics with progress bar
- Diff output for assertion failures

#### 4. Shutdown Progress Indicator
Graceful shutdown visualization showing:
- Active connections draining
- Background tasks completing
- Resource cleanup stages
- Spinner/progress during wait

### Technical Considerations

#### Tree Rendering Strategy
```rust
// Use rich_rust's Tree for hierarchical data
let mut root = TreeNode::new("Middleware Stack");
root.add_child(TreeNode::new("→ RequestLogger"));
root.add_child(TreeNode::new("→ Auth"));
root.add_child(TreeNode::new("→ CORS"));
root.add_child(TreeNode::new("[Handler]"));
root.add_child(TreeNode::new("← CORS"));
root.add_child(TreeNode::new("← Auth")); 
root.add_child(TreeNode::new("← RequestLogger"));
```

#### Progress Bar for Shutdown
```rust
// Shutdown progress with spinner
let bar = ProgressBar::new()
    .total(active_connections)
    .completed(drained)
    .width(40);
```

### Agent Compatibility Strategy
All advanced components must:
- Output simple text lists in plain mode
- Skip spinners/animations in non-TTY environments
- Provide machine-parseable test output format (TAP/JSON optional)
- Never block on visual updates

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Middleware stack shows clear execution flow
- [ ] Dependency tree detects and highlights cycles
- [ ] Test results are visually stunning yet informative
- [ ] Shutdown progress prevents user confusion
- [ ] All components degrade gracefully for agents
- [ ] No performance impact when output is suppressed

### Dependencies
- Phase 2 complete (bd-2rso) - builds on core components
- RichOutput facade (bd-36uw) - for mode-aware output
- FastApiTheme (bd-14v4) - for consistent styling

### Tasks
1. Implement middleware stack visualization
2. Implement dependency tree display
3. Implement test results formatter
4. Implement shutdown progress indicator","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:11:52.467692680Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:22:05.918694422Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:22:05.918632145Z","close_reason":"Implemented advanced output components and exports","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lkf","depends_on_id":"bd-2rso","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-tr1u","title":"Implement component unit tests","description":"# Component Unit Tests

## Task Overview

**Note: With the updated approach, comprehensive unit tests are now included inline with each implementation task.** This task is for any additional unit tests not covered by individual tasks, plus verification that all unit tests pass together.

## Scope

1. **Verify All Inline Tests Work Together**
   - Run `cargo test --package fastapi-output` to verify all tests pass
   - Check for test isolation issues (env var leaks, global state)
   - Ensure serial_test properly isolates tests

2. **Cross-Component Unit Tests**
   - Tests that involve multiple components working together
   - Tests for shared utilities used by multiple components

3. **Test Coverage Analysis**
   - Generate coverage report
   - Identify any untested code paths
   - Add tests for uncovered paths

## Test Execution Script

```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# scripts/run_unit_tests.sh

echo "=== FastAPI Output Unit Tests ==="
echo "Date: $(date)"

# Clean environment
unset CLAUDE_CODE CODEX_CLI CI NO_COLOR FASTAPI_OUTPUT_MODE

# Run all unit tests with verbose output
echo "[UNIT] Running all unit tests..."
cargo test --package fastapi-output -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee unit_tests.log

# Count results
PASSED=$(grep -c "^test .* ok$" unit_tests.log || echo 0)
FAILED=$(grep -c "^test .* FAILED$" unit_tests.log || echo 0)

echo ""
echo "=== Unit Test Summary ==="
echo "Passed: $PASSED"
echo "Failed: $FAILED"

if [ "$FAILED" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Failed tests:"
    grep "^test .* FAILED" unit_tests.log
    exit 1
fi
```

## Cross-Component Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod cross_component_tests {
    use crate::prelude::*;
    use crate::testing::*;
    use serial_test::serial;

    #[test]
    #[serial]
    fn test_all_components_use_same_mode() {
        std::env::set_var("CI", "true");
        
        let mode = OutputMode::auto();
        assert_eq\!(mode, OutputMode::Plain);
        
        // All components should render in plain mode
        let captured = capture(mode, || {
            let output = RichOutput::auto();
            output.success("Test");
            output.rule(Some("Section"));
            
            let banner = BannerConfig::new().version("1.0");
            print_startup_banner(banner, &output);
        });
        
        assert_no_ansi(&captured);
        std::env::remove_var("CI");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_theme_applies_to_all_components() {
        let theme = FastApiTheme::default();
        let output = RichOutput::builder()
            .mode(OutputMode::Plain)
            .theme(theme)
            .build();
        
        // Theme should be accessible
        assert\!(\!output.theme().success_hex().is_empty());
    }
}
```

## Coverage Report

After all tests pass, generate coverage:

```bash
cargo install cargo-tarpaulin
cargo tarpaulin --package fastapi-output --out Html
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All inline unit tests from implementation tasks pass
- [ ] No test isolation issues (env var leaks)
- [ ] Cross-component tests pass
- [ ] Test execution script works
- [ ] Coverage report generated
- [ ] >80% code coverage achieved","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:20:54.853972703Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T19:58:16.986997263Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T19:58:16.986949914Z","close_reason":"COMPLETED: 157 unit tests covering all components.

## Module Coverage:
- banner: 8 tests (plain/rich mode, config, builder)
- errors: 10 tests (validation, HTTP errors, formatters)
- logging: 9 tests (HTTP methods, entries, timing)
- routes: 7 tests (display, config options)
- middleware_stack: 9 tests (rendering, config)
- dependency_tree: 2 tests (tree, cycles)
- shutdown_progress: 2 tests (phases)
- test_results: 2 tests (TAP, plain)
- detection: 21 tests (agents, CI, overrides)
- facade: 14 tests (builder, modes)
- mode: 31 tests (parsing, indicators)
- themes: 25 tests (colors, presets)
- testing: 7 tests (capture, assertions)

## Isolation: serial_test for env var tests
## Cross-component: Mode consistency verified

Run: cargo test --package fastapi-output
Result: 157 passed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-urty","title":"Phase 5: OpenAPI Display & Visual Polish","description":"## Feature: OpenAPI Display & Visual Polish

### Parent Epic
Rich Output Integration: Premium Console UI with Agent Compatibility (bd-nww8)

### Overview
This phase focuses on OpenAPI schema visualization and overall visual polish across the framework, ensuring consistent premium aesthetics throughout.

### Background & Reasoning
OpenAPI documentation is a key feature of FastAPI-style frameworks. Visual display of the schema during development helps:
- Verify endpoint documentation is correct
- Debug schema generation issues
- Understand API structure at a glance

Additionally, this phase addresses visual consistency and polish across all components.

### Components in This Phase

#### 1. OpenAPI Schema Display
Visual representation of generated OpenAPI schema showing:
- Endpoint summaries
- Request/response schemas
- Authentication requirements
- Example values

#### 2. Schema Type Visualization  
Display complex JSON Schema types:
- Object properties
- Array types
- Enum values
- Nested schemas

#### 3. Console Theme Refinement
Polish the theme system:
- Consistent color palette
- Box style consistency
- Spacing and alignment
- Dark/light theme variants

#### 4. Help & Usage Display
Beautiful help text and usage information:
- Command-line argument help
- Configuration options
- Quick reference guides

### Technical Considerations

#### OpenAPI Display Strategy
```rust
// Show endpoint summary table
┌──────────────────── API Endpoints ────────────────────┐
│ Method │ Path              │ Summary                  │
├────────┼───────────────────┼──────────────────────────┤
│ GET    │ /users            │ List all users           │
│ POST   │ /users            │ Create a new user        │
│ GET    │ /users/{id}       │ Get user by ID           │
│ PUT    │ /users/{id}       │ Update user              │
│ DELETE │ /users/{id}       │ Delete user              │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

// Show schema for selected endpoint
┌─────────── POST /users ───────────┐
│ Create a new user                 │
│                                   │
│ Request Body (application/json)   │
│ ┌───────────────────────────────┐ │
│ │ {                             │ │
│ │   "name": string (required)  │ │
│ │   "email": string (required) │ │
│ │   "age": integer             │ │
│ │ }                             │ │
│ └───────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                   │
│ Responses                         │
│ • 201: User created               │
│ • 400: Validation error           │
│ • 409: User already exists        │
└───────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Success Criteria
- [ ] OpenAPI endpoints display in formatted table
- [ ] Schema types render clearly
- [ ] Consistent visual style across all components
- [ ] Help text is beautiful and informative
- [ ] All displays respect agent mode

### Dependencies
- Phase 4 complete (bd-1hu2) - builds on HTTP integration
- RichOutput facade (bd-36uw)
- FastApiTheme (bd-14v4)

### Tasks
1. Implement OpenAPI endpoint table display
2. Implement schema type visualization
3. Refine console theme and polish
4. Implement help and usage display","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:17:33.995427041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:41:18.423176265Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:41:18.423098811Z","close_reason":"Phase 5 complete: OpenAPI display (endpoint tables, schema visualization) and help display components implemented with all three output modes","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-urty","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ycu4","title":"Implement debug logging infrastructure for rich output","description":"# Implement Debug Logging Infrastructure

## Task Overview

Add comprehensive debug logging throughout the rich output system to aid in debugging
mode detection, output rendering, and integration issues.

## Why Debug Logging?

When things go wrong (wrong mode detected, unexpected output, etc.), we need to be able to:
- See what mode was detected and why
- Track which environment variables were checked
- Understand the rendering path taken
- Verify output was captured correctly in tests

## File: crates/fastapi-output/src/debug.rs

```rust
//\! Debug logging infrastructure for rich output.
//\!
//\! This module provides verbose debug logging that can be enabled via
//\! FASTAPI_OUTPUT_DEBUG=1 to help diagnose mode detection and rendering issues.

use std::env;
use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, Ordering};

/// Global flag for debug logging.
static DEBUG_ENABLED: AtomicBool = AtomicBool::new(false);

/// Initialize debug logging from environment.
pub fn init() {
    let enabled = env::var("FASTAPI_OUTPUT_DEBUG")
        .map(|v| v == "1" || v.to_lowercase() == "true")
        .unwrap_or(false);
    DEBUG_ENABLED.store(enabled, Ordering::SeqCst);
    if enabled {
        eprintln\!("[FASTAPI_OUTPUT] Debug logging enabled");
    }
}

/// Check if debug logging is enabled.
pub fn is_debug_enabled() -> bool {
    DEBUG_ENABLED.load(Ordering::SeqCst)
}

/// Enable debug logging programmatically (for tests).
pub fn enable_debug() {
    DEBUG_ENABLED.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);
}

/// Disable debug logging programmatically (for tests).
pub fn disable_debug() {
    DEBUG_ENABLED.store(false, Ordering::SeqCst);
}

/// Log a debug message if debug logging is enabled.
#[macro_export]
macro_rules\! debug_log {
    ($($arg:tt)*) => {
        if $crate::debug::is_debug_enabled() {
            eprintln\!("[FASTAPI_OUTPUT] {}", format\!($($arg)*));
        }
    };
}

/// Log detection-related debug info.
#[macro_export]
macro_rules\! debug_detection {
    ($($arg:tt)*) => {
        if $crate::debug::is_debug_enabled() {
            eprintln\!("[FASTAPI_OUTPUT:DETECT] {}", format\!($($arg)*));
        }
    };
}

/// Log mode-related debug info.
#[macro_export]
macro_rules\! debug_mode {
    ($($arg:tt)*) => {
        if $crate::debug::is_debug_enabled() {
            eprintln\!("[FASTAPI_OUTPUT:MODE] {}", format\!($($arg)*));
        }
    };
}

/// Log rendering-related debug info.
#[macro_export]
macro_rules\! debug_render {
    ($($arg:tt)*) => {
        if $crate::debug::is_debug_enabled() {
            eprintln\!("[FASTAPI_OUTPUT:RENDER] {}", format\!($($arg)*));
        }
    };
}

/// Log test-related debug info.
#[macro_export]
macro_rules\! debug_test {
    ($($arg:tt)*) => {
        if $crate::debug::is_debug_enabled() {
            eprintln\!("[FASTAPI_OUTPUT:TEST] {}", format\!($($arg)*));
        }
    };
}
```

## Usage Examples

### In detection.rs

```rust
use crate::{debug_detection, debug_log};

pub fn is_agent_environment() -> bool {
    debug_detection\!("Checking agent environment...");
    
    for var in AGENT_ENV_VARS {
        if env::var(var).is_ok() {
            debug_detection\!("Found agent env var: {}", var);
            return true;
        }
    }
    
    debug_detection\!("No agent env vars found");
    false
}
```

### In mode.rs

```rust
use crate::debug_mode;

impl OutputMode {
    pub fn auto() -> Self {
        debug_mode\!("OutputMode::auto() called");
        
        if let Ok(mode_str) = env::var("FASTAPI_OUTPUT_MODE") {
            debug_mode\!("Found FASTAPI_OUTPUT_MODE={}", mode_str);
            if let Ok(mode) = mode_str.parse() {
                debug_mode\!("Parsed mode: {:?}", mode);
                return mode;
            }
        }
        
        let detected = is_agent_environment();
        debug_mode\!("Agent detection result: {}", detected);
        
        if detected {
            debug_mode\!("Using Plain mode (agent detected)");
            OutputMode::Plain
        } else {
            debug_mode\!("Using Rich mode (human terminal)");
            OutputMode::Rich
        }
    }
}
```

### In facade.rs

```rust
use crate::debug_render;

impl RichOutput {
    pub fn success(&self, message: &str) {
        debug_render\!("success() mode={:?} msg={}", self.mode, message);
        self.status(StatusKind::Success, message);
    }
    
    fn write_line(&self, text: &str) {
        debug_render\!("write_line() len={}", text.len());
        // ...
    }
}
```

### In tests

```rust
use crate::{debug_test, debug};

#[test]
fn test_with_debug() {
    debug::enable_debug();
    debug_test\!("Starting test_with_debug");
    
    let mode = OutputMode::auto();
    debug_test\!("Got mode: {:?}", mode);
    
    // Test assertions...
    
    debug::disable_debug();
}
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Effect |
|----------|--------|
| `FASTAPI_OUTPUT_DEBUG=1` | Enable all debug logging |
| `FASTAPI_OUTPUT_DEBUG=true` | Enable all debug logging |
| Unset/other | Debug logging disabled |

## Unit Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_debug_disabled_by_default() {
        disable_debug();
        assert\!(\!is_debug_enabled());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_enable_disable_debug() {
        disable_debug();
        assert\!(\!is_debug_enabled());
        
        enable_debug();
        assert\!(is_debug_enabled());
        
        disable_debug();
        assert\!(\!is_debug_enabled());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_debug_log_macro_when_disabled() {
        disable_debug();
        // Should not panic or print
        debug_log\!("This should not appear");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_debug_log_macro_when_enabled() {
        enable_debug();
        // Should print to stderr
        debug_log\!("Test message: {}", 42);
        disable_debug();
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_all_debug_macros() {
        enable_debug();
        debug_detection\!("detection test");
        debug_mode\!("mode test");
        debug_render\!("render test");
        debug_test\!("test test");
        disable_debug();
    }
}
```

## Integration with Other Modules

All modules should use the debug macros:
- detection.rs: debug_detection\!
- mode.rs: debug_mode\!
- facade.rs: debug_render\!
- components/*.rs: debug_render\!
- testing.rs: debug_test\!

- [ ] All debug macros working
- [ ] Environment variable detection works
- [ ] Programmatic enable/disable works
- [ ] All other modules use appropriate debug macros
- [ ] Debug output goes to stderr only

 {"id":"bd-zgme","title":"Implement dependency tree display","description":"## Task: Dependency Tree Display

### Parent Feature
Phase 3: Advanced Output Components (bd-1lkf)

### Overview
Create a visual tree display of the dependency injection graph, showing how dependencies are resolved and detecting circular dependencies.

### Background & Reasoning
Dependency injection can become complex in larger applications. Visualizing the dependency graph helps developers:
- Understand what depends on what
- Identify circular dependency issues before runtime
- Optimize resolution order
- Debug 'missing dependency' errors by seeing the full chain

### Technical Implementation

#### Data Structures
```rust
// crates/fastapi-output/src/components/dependency_tree.rs

pub struct DependencyNode {
    pub name: String,
    pub type_name: String,
    pub scope: DependencyScope,
    pub children: Vec<DependencyNode>,
    pub is_circular_ref: bool,
}

pub enum DependencyScope {
    Singleton,   // Application lifetime
    Request,     // Per-request
    Transient,   // New instance each time
}

pub struct DependencyTreeDisplay {
    roots: Vec<DependencyNode>,
    show_types: bool,
    show_scopes: bool,
    highlight_cycles: bool,
}
```

#### Visualization

**Rich Mode (Tree with Colors)**:
```
┌────────────── Dependency Graph ──────────────┐
│                                               │
│ DatabasePool (singleton)                      │
│ └── type: sqlx::Pool<Postgres>               │
│                                               │
│ UserService (request)                         │
│ ├── DatabasePool                              │
│ └── CacheService                              │
│     └── RedisPool (singleton)                 │
│                                               │
│ AuthService (request)                         │
│ ├── UserService                               │
│ └── TokenValidator (singleton)                │
│                                               │
│ ⚠ CYCLE DETECTED:                            │
│   ServiceA → ServiceB → ServiceA              │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Plain Mode**:
```
Dependency Graph:
  DatabasePool (singleton)
  UserService (request) <- DatabasePool, CacheService
  CacheService (request) <- RedisPool
  AuthService (request) <- UserService, TokenValidator
  WARNING: Circular dependency: ServiceA -> ServiceB -> ServiceA
```

#### Implementation
```rust
impl DependencyTreeDisplay {
    pub fn display(&self) {
        let output = RichOutput::global();
        
        if !output.should_render() {
            self.display_plain(&output);
            return;
        }
        
        let theme = output.theme();
        let mut tree_nodes = Vec::new();
        
        for root in &self.roots {
            tree_nodes.push(self.build_tree_node(root, theme, 0));
        }
        
        // Check for cycles
        let cycles = self.detect_cycles();
        
        let mut content_parts: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
        
        for node in tree_nodes {
            let tree = Tree::new(node);
            content_parts.push(format!("{}", tree.render_to_string()));
        }
        
        if !cycles.is_empty() {
            content_parts.push(String::new());
            content_parts.push(theme.style_text("⚠ CYCLES DETECTED:", "red bold"));
            for cycle in cycles {
                content_parts.push(format!("  {}", cycle.join(" → ")));
            }
        }
        
        let panel = Panel::from_text(&content_parts.join("\n"))
            .title("Dependency Graph")
            .rounded();
        
        output.renderable(&panel);
    }
    
    fn build_tree_node(&self, dep: &DependencyNode, theme: &FastApiTheme, depth: usize) -> TreeNode {
        let scope_indicator = match dep.scope {
            DependencyScope::Singleton => theme.style_text("(singleton)", "blue"),
            DependencyScope::Request => theme.style_text("(request)", "green"),
            DependencyScope::Transient => theme.style_text("(transient)", "yellow"),
        };
        
        let label = if dep.is_circular_ref {
            theme.style_text(&format!("{} {} ↻", dep.name, scope_indicator), "red")
        } else {
            format!("{} {}", dep.name, scope_indicator)
        };
        
        let mut node = TreeNode::new(label);
        
        if self.show_types {
            node.add_child(TreeNode::new(
                theme.style_text(&format!("type: {}", dep.type_name), "dim")
            ));
        }
        
        for child in &dep.children {
            node.add_child(self.build_tree_node(child, theme, depth + 1));
        }
        
        node
    }
    
    fn detect_cycles(&self) -> Vec<Vec<String>> {
        // DFS-based cycle detection
        let mut cycles = Vec::new();
        let mut visited = HashSet::new();
        let mut path = Vec::new();
        
        for root in &self.roots {
            self.find_cycles(root, &mut visited, &mut path, &mut cycles);
        }
        
        cycles
    }
}
```

### Integration with DI System
```rust
// In fastapi-core/src/depends.rs

impl DependencyContainer {
    pub fn display_graph(&self) {
        let roots = self.build_dependency_nodes();
        
        DependencyTreeDisplay {
            roots,
            show_types: true,
            show_scopes: true,
            highlight_cycles: true,
        }.display();
    }
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DependencyNode and DependencyTreeDisplay structs
- [ ] Recursive tree building with depth tracking
- [ ] Scope coloring (singleton=blue, request=green, transient=yellow)
- [ ] Circular dependency detection with DFS
- [ ] Cycle highlighting in red with ↻ indicator
- [ ] Type information display (optional)
- [ ] Plain text fallback
- [ ] Integration with DependencyContainer
- [ ] Unit tests for cycle detection

### Dependencies
- bd-36uw (RichOutput facade)
- bd-14v4 (FastApiTheme)
- Access to fastapi-core DependencyContainer

### Considerations
- Deep dependency trees should be truncated with '...' indicator
- Consider caching the graph visualization for large apps
- Cycle detection must handle self-referential deps","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:48.877004850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:28:31.691597921Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:28:31.691509544Z","close_reason":"Implemented - dependency_tree.rs complete with DependencyNode, DependencyTreeDisplay, cycle detection, and tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zgme","depends_on_id":"bd-14v4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-zgme","depends_on_id":"bd-1lkf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-zgme","depends_on_id":"bd-36uw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-bss","title":"Dependency caching with request scope","description":"Implement request-scoped dependency caching:

BEHAVIOR (per FastAPI spec):
- use_cache: bool = true (default)
- Cache key: (callable, sorted_scopes, computed_scope)
- Same dependency called twice = same instance

CACHE LOCATIONS:
- 'request' scope: Cached for entire request
- 'function' scope: Fresh each call (no caching)

STORAGE:
- Store in RequestContext or dedicated DependencyCache
- TypeId-based lookup for type safety
- Clear on request completion

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- use_cache=true returns same instance
- use_cache=false returns fresh instance
- Scope rules enforced
- No memory leaks

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Caching behavior
- Unit: Cache key generation
- Unit: Scope isolation
- Integration: Complex dependency graphs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:11:08.615093525Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:01:56.227231922Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:01:56.226790510Z","close_reason":"Already fully implemented - DependencyCache, DependencyScope, DependsConfig, NoCache all in place with comprehensive tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["caching","di","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-bss","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-aha","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}

-{"id":"fastapi_rust-c75","title":"Parameter alias support","description":"Implement parameter aliasing:

ALIAS TYPES (per FastAPI):
- alias: Alternative name in request
- validation_alias: Name for validation
- serialization_alias: Name for serialization

HEADER EXAMPLE:
#[param(alias = 'X-Custom-Token')]
token: String
// Looks for header 'X-Custom-Token' instead of 'token'

QUERY EXAMPLE:
#[param(alias = 'q')]
query: String
// Accepts ?q=value instead of ?query=value

ALIAS PROPAGATION:
if alias is set and serialization_alias is None:
    serialization_alias = alias
if alias is set and validation_alias is None:
    validation_alias = alias

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Alias used for extraction
- Alias appears in OpenAPI
- Propagation rules work
- Works with all param types

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Header alias
- Unit: Query alias
- Unit: Propagation rules
- OpenAPI: Alias in schema","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:13:51.872134286Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T15:13:51.872134286Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alias","params","phase-2"]}
